title: Questions from Quarantine: The Need for a Coordinated National Response
summary: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday and if you have a question that you want answered tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine.
body: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday and if you have a question that you want answered tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article introduces a weekly web series titled 'Questions from Quarantine,' hosted by Juliette Kayyem and Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux, which aims to provide public guidance during a global health crisis. It emphasizes the need for a coordinated national response to such crises, inviting public participation through social media with a specific hashtag. The content is framed within the broader work of the Security and Global Health Project, highlighting its focus on public health security and crisis management. The piece also references related expert analyses on topics like mega-event safety, supply chains, and potential migration crises, situating the discussion within ongoing policy and technological considerations for global health emergencies.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为“隔离问题”的每周网络系列节目，由朱丽叶·凯耶姆和玛格丽特·布尔多博士主持，旨在在全球健康危机期间为公众提供指导。文章强调了对此类危机需要协调的国家响应，并通过特定标签邀请公众在社交媒体上参与。内容被置于“安全与全球健康项目”的更广泛工作中，突出了其对公共卫生安全和危机管理的关注。文章还引用了相关专家对大型活动安全、供应链以及潜在移民危机等主题的分析，将讨论置于全球卫生紧急事件持续的政策和技术考量背景之下。
